items.title: Remove password from `Authentication` debug output
items.body: This will prevent users of our program from making mistakes, it also follows the other part of monero-oxide that attempt to hide information from the debug output that might be private.

items.title: Sensitive Data Leakage via derive(Debug) in Simple Request Transport
items.body: ### Description In `interface/daemon/simple-request/src/lib.rs`, there is an inconsistency in how sensitive data is handled within debug outputs. While the `Authentication` enum has a manual `fmt::Debug` implementation to prevent credential leaking, the parent struct `SimpleRequestTransport` still uses `#[derive(Debug)]` on line 54. ### Vulnerability Detail The `SimpleRequestTransport` struct contains an `authentication` field of type `Authentication`. Because the struct itself derives `Debug`, any debug-level logging or printing of the transport object (e.g., `println!("{:?}", transport)`) will include and expose the authentication credentials. This effectively bypasses the redaction logic implemented in the `Authentication` enum's manual Debug trait. ### Impact This leads to **Sensitive Information Leakage**. Credentials (username/password) could be inadvertently written to system logs or console outputs, especially during debugging or automated logging in production environments. ### Recommended Fix Remove `Debug` from the `derive` attribute of `Simple Request Transport` and implement the `Debug` trait manually to ensure the `authentication` field is either redacted or skipped entirely.

items.title: `monero-address` `from_str` method of `Address` is not idiomatic.
items.body:
The [Address](https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/blob/cae84b7b16051ac8bf92ed151aad49b9e6f4fcae/monero-oxide/wallet/address/src/lib.rs#L350) struct has a method named [from_str](https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/blob/cae84b7b16051ac8bf92ed151aad49b9e6f4fcae/monero-oxide/wallet/address/src/lib.rs#L472) to construct itself from a &str and a second parameter.

It also has a method named [from_str_with_unchecked_network](https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/blob/cae84b7b16051ac8bf92ed151aad49b9e6f4fcae/monero-oxide/wallet/address/src/lib.rs#L419) that construct itself from a &str only.

The trait [FromStr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/str/trait.FromStr.html) from the standard library has a method named [from_str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/str/trait.FromStr.html#tymethod.from_str) that construct the type from &str only.

From a Rust design pattern perspective, I suggest the following:

- rename `from_str` to `from_str_with_checked_network`
- implements `FromStr` for `Address`.

I can create a PR if agreed.

items.title: monero-simple-request-rpc is variable-time w.r.t. the authentication
items.body: Please see https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/116 for the vibe gestured at. There are more specific issues too however.

items.title: Exorbitant fee returned from Monero node may cause overflow on multiplication
items.body: https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/blob/dc8e38c153e463eb680ec008cde9cff51ed29926/monero-oxide/rpc/src/lib.rs#L758 for the problematic line.

items.title: Expose webpki flag on monero-simple-request-rpc
items.body:
I have encountered the following panic in production.

```
thread 'main' panicked at /root/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/simple-request-0.1.0/src/lib.rs:65:8:
asb             | couldn't fetch system's SSL roots: Custom { kind: NotFound, error: "no native root CA certificates found (errors: [])" }
asb             | note: run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` environment variable to display a backtrace
```

This should be replicable using [this](https://github.com/eigenwallet/core/blob/36c1c3a28be6c9e0e01878b3bcc9175a1ae52c5c/swap-asb/Dockerfile) Docker Image. I fixed it in our case by  properly installing the root certificates in the Docker image (https://github.com/eigenwallet/core/pull/569)

`monero-simple-request-rpc` should expose a `webpki` flag which when enabled will use the Mozilla root certificates as defined here: https://github.com/rustls/hyper-rustls/blob/5e9b69b4128abfc147e8b6fa55977eac4c3f575c/src/config.rs#L56C8-L56C25

This "issue" is also tracked here https://github.com/rustls/hyper-rustls/issues/187 but it seems to be expected behaviour.

items.title: Fast calls for FCMP++ proof size
items.body: I opened [this PR](https://github.com/kayabaNerve/fcmp-plus-plus/pull/29) to the original FCMP++ repo to avoid the slow call for proof size during proof de-serialization. @kayabaNerve NACK'd that PR in favor of implementing a table for proof sizes internal to the rust FCMP++ lib. We have a static table in our branch on the C++ side over [here](https://github.com/seraphis-migration/monero/blob/15edaa505908a377f0b013d3a522a966278865fa/src/fcmp_pp/proof_len.h). I figured letting the caller handling tabling is nice, but I'm ok with an alternative solution that achieves fast calls for the proof len.

items.title: Move monero-rpc over to cuprate-epee-encoding
items.body:
When I wrote the RPC code originally present in monero-serai, I started with [monero-epee-bin-serde](https://github.com/monero-rs/monero-epee-bin-serde), which had [multiple fundamental issues](https://github.com/monero-rs/monero-epee-bin-serde/issues?q=is%3Aissue). Writing a good epee library is _hard_. It's an format which originally lacked documentation.

In order to deal with how monero-epee-bin-serde had too many issues, and without fixing the issues within monero-epee-bin-serde due to the difficulty of doing so, I simply avoided epee as much as possible. This worked entirely fine, albeit inefficiently, except for one location. Fetching outputs' indexes fundamentally requires epee as the Monero node _only_ offers a binary endpoint. This led to me writing the following:

https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/blob/32e6b5fe5ba9e1ea3e68da882550005122a11d22/monero-oxide/rpc/src/lib.rs#L838-L1016

The above monstrosity, fragile and should never have been born, _works_. It may not work well, it may not work in the future, but it works _now_.

Ideally, we would be able to replace this with monero-epee-bin-serde for a guarantee of quality, and _start_ using epee where we prior avoided it (for efficiency reasons).

The primary question is if `monero-epee-bin-serde` is worth the additional dependency tree given the benefit _to this spot_. If these horrific 178 lines work without issue, then there's no benefit. Additionally, with FCMP++, we could _reduce_ the scope of monero-rpc and _remove_ `get_o_indexes` as it'll be unnecessary to satisfy monero-wallet.

Technically, another crate can define a struct and implement `monero_rpc::Rpc` itself _with epee internally_ for those who prefer the larger dependency tree for the stability offered. That's due to the modular design intended with it. Unfortunately, it's difficult in practice, as I commented [here](https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/34#issuecomment-3217101957). Discussions on an appropriate re-ar

commit.message:
New `monero-rpc` candidate (#66)

* Split `ProvidesTransactions` out of `Rpc`

Resolves https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/3.

Candidate for the rewrite discussed in
https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/34.

* Split `ProvidesBlockchain` out of `Rpc`

Introduces APIs to get blocks in batches, resolving the originally stated issue
of https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/34.

* Move `generate_blocks` from `Rpc` to `MoneroDaemon`

* Introduce `ProvidesBlockchainMeta`, `PublishTransaction`

* Split out the fee API from `Rpc`

Fixes https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/65.

* Move `get_o_indexes` to `ProvidesOutputs`

* `ProvidesScannableBlocks` abstraction

Not currently implemented as it practically requires `get_blocks.bin`, which we
don't support.

* Trim `get_output_distribution_end_height` in favor of `ProvidesBlockchainMeta`

Shrinks `ProvidesBlockchainMeta` by removing `get_hardfork_version` and moving
`get_block_hash` to `ProvidesBlockchain`.

* Move `DecoyRpc::get_outs` to `ProvidesOutputs::get_ringct_outputs`

* ProvidesDecoys

* Remove `get_` prefix from RPC methods

* Rework erros in `monero-rpc`

* Remove untrusted input from error messages

This ensures they're safe to log.

* Bound HTTP response sizes to prevent a malicious Monero node which actually replies with a several GB response

* Correct rebase

Effectively re-applies 75a5048bc6e8ff7012eeb8f208716e16979323ba and
b8263ce4b04ad8cc018a30bcacabc24c7ab94246.

* Fix `no-std` builds of `monero-rpc`

* Attempt fix for prunable hashes not lining up

* Clarify bounds in the `monero_daemon` module

* Rename `monero-rpc` to `monero-interface`

This is no longer solely for _Remote_ Procedure Calls, and `monero-rpc` is
already a published crate.

* Rename "Source" terminology to "Interface"

* Move `interface/src/monero_daemon.rs` to `interface/daemon`

It has almost no relation to the rest of the library and we aren't benefited by
its inclusion.

Rem

Extensively introduce internal bounds (#77)

* Extensively introduce internal bounds

Resolves https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/72.

This commit's most notable changes are to `monero-io`, which has been widened
to accept `FnMut` over `Fn` (as all `Fn` are `FnMut`), and to the handling of
arbitrary data as defined in `monero-wallet`.

Prior, `monero-wallet` would only create transactions whose extra was less than
the limit imposed by a relay limit, yet would accept arbitrary data from an
extra of any size. Now, when parsing arbitrary data out of a transaction, only
the arbitrary data fields within the first 1060 bytes are considered. This lets
us establish a bound on the amount of arbitrary data despite extra itself being
unbounded. The serialization of `Metadata` has also been adjusted to use a
`VarInt` over a `u64`.

The bump to the version of `std-shims` is required for
`impl<R: Read> Read for &mut R`, which is used inside the new
`Transaction::read` function (which now enforces a maximum byte length on
non-miner transactions).

Split out of https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/pull/66.
Specifically, this represents the following commits:

commit e041ad78e1c209ba5a66e7f62f3bd87ae650093e
Fix `no-std` builds

---

commit 2ab2db1bce641f1610dd7729fdcc4542d8b7f55d
Update citation for the TX extra relay rule now that it's merged

Also adds a test for the new limit on arbitrary data, and prefers `_SIZE` to
`_LEN` in constants I've declared.

---

commit 57d5bca53f2ec710539ee790cd8a7798669a07df
Bound the size of addresses when deserializing a `SignableTransaction`

---

commit 63cc9361403bc9b6d7837eea531fe208894154d4
Introduce length bound on the size of an address string with `SignableTransaction` deserialization

---

commit 1eebe1949d9af58971ac0a92d106ceb747cb61a3
Rename `MAX_INPUTS` to `INPUTS_UPPER_BOUND`, as it isn't accurate

Also uses it within `SignableTransaction` deserialization.

---

commit 3f03b0f9a8042ac2f58cfcd52166a8768c7b852e
I

From a Rust design pattern perspective, I suggest the following:

- rename `from_str` to `from_str_with_checked_network`
- implements `FromStr` for `Address`.

I can create a PR if agreed.

commit.message:
Extensively introduce internal bounds (#77)

* Extensively introduce internal bounds

Resolves https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/72.

This commit's most notable changes are to `monero-io`, which has been widened
to accept `FnMut` over `Fn` (as all `Fn` are `FnMut`), and to the handling of
arbitrary data as defined in `monero-wallet`.

Prior, `monero-wallet` would only create transactions whose extra was less than
the limit imposed by a relay limit, yet would accept arbitrary data from an
extra of any size. Now, when parsing arbitrary data out of a transaction, only
the arbitrary data fields within the first 1060 bytes are considered. This lets
us establish a bound on the amount of arbitrary data despite extra itself being
unbounded. The serialization of `Metadata` has also been adjusted to use a
`VarInt` over a `u64`.

The bump to the version of `std-shims` is required for
`impl<R: Read> Read for &mut R`, which is used inside the new
`Transaction::read` function (which now enforces a maximum byte length on
non-miner transactions).

Split out of https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/pull/66.
Specifically, this represents the following commits:

commit e041ad78e1c209ba5a66e7f62f3bd87ae650093e
Fix `no-std` builds

---

commit 2ab2db1bce641f1610dd7729fdcc4542d8b7f55d
Update citation for the TX extra relay rule now that it's merged

Also adds a test for the new limit on arbitrary data, and prefers `_SIZE` to
`_LEN` in constants I've declared.

---

commit 57d5bca53f2ec710539ee790cd8a7798669a07df
Bound the size of addresses when deserializing a `SignableTransaction`

---

commit 63cc9361403bc9b6d7837eea531fe208894154d4
Introduce length bound on the size of an address string with `SignableTransaction` deserialization

---

commit 1eebe1949d9af58971ac0a92d106ceb747cb61a3
Rename `MAX_INPUTS` to `INPUTS_UPPER_BOUND`, as it isn't accurate

Also uses it within `SignableTransaction` deserialization.

---

items.title: Sensitive Data Leakage via derive(Debug) in Simple Request Transport
items.body: ### Description In `interface/daemon/simple-request/src/lib.rs`, there is an inconsistency in how sensitive data is handled within debug outputs. While the `Authentication` enum has a manual `fmt::Debug` implementation to prevent credential leaking, the parent struct `SimpleRequestTransport` still uses `#[derive(Debug)]` on line 54. ### Vulnerability Detail The `SimpleRequestTransport` struct contains an `authentication` field of type `Authentication`. Because the struct itself derives `Debug`, any debug-level logging or printing of the transport object (e.g., `println!("{:?}", transport)`) will include and expose the authentication credentials. This effectively bypasses the redaction logic implemented in the `Authentication` enum's manual Debug trait. ### Impact This leads to **Sensitive Information Leakage**. Credentials (username/password) could be inadvertently written to system logs or console outputs, especially during debugging or automated logging in production environments. ### Recommended Fix Remove `Debug` from the `derive` attribute of `Simple Request Transport` and implement the `Debug` trait manually to ensure the `authentication` field is either redacted or skipped entirely.

New `monero-rpc` candidate (#66)

* Split `ProvidesTransactions` out of `Rpc`

Resolves https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/3.

Candidate for the rewrite discussed in
https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/34.

* Split `ProvidesBlockchain` out of `Rpc`

Introduces APIs to get blocks in batches, resolving the originally stated issue
of https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/34.

* Move `generate_blocks` from `Rpc` to `MoneroDaemon`

* Introduce `ProvidesBlockchainMeta`, `PublishTransaction`

* Split out the fee API from `Rpc`

Fixes https://github.com/monero-oxide/monero-oxide/issues/65.

* Move `get_o_indexes` to `ProvidesOutputs`

* `ProvidesScannableBlocks` abstraction

Not currently implemented as it practically requires `get_blocks.bin`, which we
don't support.

* Trim `get_output_distribution_end_height` in favor of `ProvidesBlockchainMeta`

Shrinks `ProvidesBlockchainMeta` by removing `get_hardfork_version` and moving
`get_block_hash` to `ProvidesBlockchain`.

* Move `DecoyRpc::get_outs` to `ProvidesOutputs::get_ringct_outputs`

* ProvidesDecoys

* Remove `get_` prefix from RPC methods

* Rework erros in `monero-rpc`

* Remove untrusted input from error messages

This ensures they're safe to log.

* Bound HTTP response sizes to prevent a malicious Monero node which actually replies with a several GB response

* Correct rebase

Effectively re-applies 75a5048bc6e8ff7012eeb8f208716e16979323ba and
b8263ce4b04ad8cc018a30bcacabc24c7ab94246.

* Fix `no-std` builds of `monero-rpc`

* Attempt fix for prunable hashes not lining up

* Clarify bounds in the `monero_daemon` module

* Rename `monero-rpc` to `monero-interface`

This is no longer solely for _Remote_ Procedure Calls, and `monero-rpc` is
already a published crate.

* Rename "Source" terminology to "Interface"

* Move `interface/src/monero_daemon.rs` to `interface/daemon`

It has almost no relation to the rest of the library and we aren't benefited by
its inclusion.
